examples.title: Compute Surface Normal
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate the face normal of a polygon
examples.code:
FUNCTION ComputeFaceNormal(v1, v2, v3):
 // Compute two edge vectors
 edge1 = Subtract(v2, v1)
 edge2 = Subtract(v3, v1)
 
 // Cross product to get raw normal
 normalRaw = CrossProduct(edge1, edge2)
 
 // Normalize to unit vector
 length = Magnitude(normalRaw)
 normal = Divide(normalRaw, length)
 
 RETURN normal
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Takes three vertices of a polygon, computes edge vectors, takes their cross product to get perpendicular direction, then normalizes to unit length.

examples.title: Phong Illumination Model
examples.description: Implementation of complete Phong lighting calculation
examples.code:
FUNCTION PhongIllumination(point, normal, material, lights, viewDir):
 resultIntensity = 0.0
 
 // Ambient component
 resultIntensity += material.ambientCoeff * lights.ambientIntensity
 
 // For each light source
 FOR EACH light IN lights:
 lightDir = Normalize(light.position - point)
 
 // Diffuse component (Lambertian)
 dotNL = DotProduct(normal, lightDir)
 IF dotNL > 0 THEN
 resultIntensity += material.diffuseCoeff * light.diffuseIntensity * dotNL
 END IF
 
 // Specular component (Phong)
 reflection = Reflect(-lightDir, normal)
 dotRV = DotProduct(reflection, viewDir)
 IF dotNL > 0 AND dotRV > 0 THEN
 specular = material.specularCoeff * light.specularIntensity * (dotRV ^ material.shininess)
 resultIntensity += specular
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN resultIntensity
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Calculates final color intensity by combining ambient, diffuse (using N·L), and specular (using R·V) components with material properties.
examples.timeComplexity: O(L) where L is number of lights

examples.title: Gouraud Shading Algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM GouraudShading(polygon, vertices, light, material):
BEGIN
 // Step 1: Compute vertex normals by averaging adjacent face normals
 FOR EACH vertex IN vertices:
 vertex.normal = AverageNormalsOfAdjacentFaces(vertex)
 END FOR
 
 // Step 2: Compute intensity at each vertex using Phong model
 FOR EACH vertex IN polygon.vertices:
 vertex.intensity = PhongIllumination(vertex.position, vertex.normal, material, light)
 END FOR
 
 // Step 3: Rasterize and interpolate intensities
 FOR EACH scanLine intersecting polygon:
 // Find intersection points with polygon edges
 i1 = InterpolateIntensity(scanLine.y, edge1, vertex1.intensity, vertex2.intensity)
 i2 = InterpolateIntensity(scanLine.y, edge2, vertex2.intensity, vertex3.intensity)
 
 // Interpolate along scan line
 FOR x FROM leftEdge TO rightEdge:
 pixelIntensity = Interpolate(i1, i2, x)
 SetPixelColor(x, scanLine.y, pixelIntensity)
 END FOR
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: Computes lighting at vertices, then linearly interpolates the intensity values across the polygon during rasterization.

examples.title: Phong Shading Algorithm
examples.explanation: Interpolates normals across the polygon surface instead of intensities, then computes full lighting model at each pixel for accurate highlights.
